name: Mariner 4
first: First spacecraft to reach Mars and return close-up images of another planet
description: The mission that ended dreams of a habitable Mars. Mariner 4's 22 grainy images, taken during a 25-minute closest approach at 9,846 km altitude, showed a cratered surface resembling the Moon — no canals, no vegetation, no obvious life. The atmosphere was 100× thinner than expected. The discovery reshaped Mars in the public imagination from sister-Earth to dead world. Tracking the spacecraft also produced the first measurement of the solar wind's structure between planets.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Atlas LV-3 Agena-D lifts off from Cape Canaveral LC-12.

events.label: TCM-1
events.note: First mid-course correction completed; trajectory locked.

events.label: MARS FLYBY
events.note: Closest approach 9,846 km. 22 images returned over 8 hours during data playback.

events.label: FIRST IMAGES
events.note: First close-up images of another planet ever returned to Earth.
